metadata.title: PDF to Markdown for LLM Notes
metadata.description: Prepare copied or exported PDF text as clean Markdown for LLM prompts, RAG notes, and source-backed document workflows.

page.title: PDF to Markdown for LLM Notes

tool.title: Turn copied PDF text into Markdown for LLM workflows
tool.description: Paste text copied or exported from a PDF, clean broken paragraphs, normalize headings and lists, then copy or download a Markdown note for an LLM prompt, RAG draft, or source review.
tool.source_boundary: This browser-local page prepares text that you paste from a PDF. It does not parse encrypted, scanned, image-only, or layout-heavy PDF files, and it is not an official MarkItDown or Microsoft tool.
checklist: Use this for copied or exported text from source PDFs, not for image-only scans.
Keep source URLs, paper titles, and page numbers in your notes before sending text to an LLM.
Review the Markdown output before using it in a RAG index or prompt.
Use MarkItDown, MinerU, or another full parser when you need tables, figures, OCR, or batch conversion.

workflow.title: A cautious PDF-to-Markdown workflow
workflow.description: The first validation surface focuses on the common low-friction job: preparing extracted PDF text for LLM consumption without uploading it.

items.title: Extract text from the source PDF
items.description: Copy text from the PDF viewer or export text with a trusted parser. Keep the original title, URL, and page references nearby.

items.title: Clean paragraph breaks
items.description: Paste the text here to collapse PDF line wraps, normalize bullet lists, and turn short section labels into Markdown headings.

items.title: Review before model use
items.description: LLM-ready Markdown should preserve citations and warnings. Do not let cleanup remove source boundaries or table meaning.

items.title: Escalate when parsing matters
items.description: For scanned PDFs, tables, formulas, figures, or batch jobs, use a real document parser and treat this page as a manual review scratchpad.

comparison.title: Where this fits beside full document converters
comparison.description: The page is a small validation tool in Motubrain's document-conversion cluster, not a replacement for full PDF parsing stacks.

items.title: Good for quick copied text
items.description: Use it when the PDF viewer already gives readable text and you mainly need Markdown cleanup before an LLM prompt.

items.title: Not for OCR or layout recovery
items.description: Scans, tables, figures, math, and multi-column layouts need purpose-built PDF extraction before Markdown cleanup.

items.title: MarkItDown context
items.description: Microsoft MarkItDown and similar tools target multi-format conversion. This page documents the lightweight manual path while broader conversion tools are tested.

items.title: Developer tool path
items.description: The same document-conversion cluster can later support PDF-to-Markdown, MarkItDown MCP, and LLM document conversion pages only after GSC and event proof appears.

faq.title: PDF to Markdown FAQ
faq.description: Scope and safety notes for this bounded validation tool.

items.question: Can this page upload and convert any PDF file?
items.answer: No. This first validation page works with text you paste from a PDF or another extractor. It does not upload files or parse PDF binaries.

items.question: Why target PDF to Markdown if the tool starts from pasted text?
items.answer: Many users already copy text from a PDF but need line-break cleanup, headings, bullets, and a Markdown download before using the text with an LLM. A full parser can be built only after this demand path shows signals.

items.question: Does Motubrain.org claim to be MarkItDown?
items.answer: No. Motubrain.org is an independent developer utility site. MarkItDown is mentioned only as context for full document conversion workflows.

items.question: What leading indicators matter for this page?
items.answer: URL-level GSC impressions for PDF-to-Markdown or LLM document conversion terms, plus document_tool_success and document_tool_download events.
